ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Look for samples of lettering in magazines and on posters.
[ ] Collect quotes, verses, poems and sayings that inspire you.

[ ] Use lettering to decorate Christmas or Valentine's cards.
[ ] Use what you are learning to create a birthday card for a friend or family member.

[ ] Purchase a small decorative chalkboard to create as a gift for a birthday, Mother's Day, or Easter.
[ ] Begin a Scripture Journal of lettering designs.
[ ] Use what you are learning to create a birthday card for a friend or family member.

resources.title: Lettering and Modern Calligraphy: A Beginner's Guide: Learn Hand Lettering and Brush Lettering
resources.author: Paper Peony Press
resources.rationale: This is a great book to help with transferring skills from the chalkboard to paper. Students will want to start using this after completing Term 1.

resources.title: Lettering with Purpose: Creative Techniques and Prompts for Making Meaningful, Inspirational Hand-lettered Art
resources.author: Brittany Luiz
resources.rationale: This is a great book to help with transferring skills from the chalkboard to paper. Students will want to start using this after completing Term 1.
